name: Britney Spears
description:
'''Britney Jean Spears''' (born December 2, 1981) is an American singer. Referred to as the "[[Honorific nicknames in popular music|Princess of Pop]]", she is widely regarded as [[Cultural impact of Britney Spears|one of the most influential entertainers of the 21st century]]. Her impact on [[pop music]]—particularly her role in reviving [[teen pop]]—and energetic stage performances contributed to her widespread success.

Spears began her professional career in 1992 as a cast member for the musical ''[[Ruthless!]]'' and the final two seasons of ''[[The All-New Mickey Mouse Club]]'' television series before signing with [[Jive Records]] in 1997. She rose to prominence with "[[...Baby One More Time]]", which became a global chart-topper and one of the [[List of best-selling singles|best-selling singles in history]]. Spears became the best-selling teenage artist of all time with the [[List of best-selling albums|best-selling albums]] ''[[...Baby One More Time (album)|...Baby One More Time]]'' (1999) and ''[[Oops!... I Did It Again (album)|Oops!... I Did It Again]]'' (2000), as well as her third album ''[[Britney (album)|Britney]]'' (2001), her [[List of best-selling albums of the 21st century|21st-century bestseller]]. After starring in the films ''[[Longshot (film)|Longshot]]'' (2001)  and ''[[Crossroads (2002 film)|Crossroads]]'' (2002), she released the albums ''[[In the Zone]]'' (2003) and ''[[Blackout (Britney Spears album)|Blackout]]'' (2007). The former spawned the top-ten single "[[Toxic (song)|Toxic]]", which won the [[Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Recording]].

Facing lifelong public scrutiny and struggles with [[Mental disorder|mental illness]], Spears was placed under a controversial conservatorship in 2008. During this period, she recorded ''[[Circus (Britney Spears album)|Circus]]'' (2008) and ''[[Femme Fatale (Britney Spears album)|Femme Fatale]]'' (2011) for Jive, and ''[[Britney Jean]]'' (2013) and ''[[Glory (Britney Spears album)|Glory]]'' (2016) for [[RCA Records]]. With the singles "[[Womanizer (song)|Womanizer]]" (2008), "[[3 (Britney Spears song)|3]]" (2009), and "[[Hold It Against Me]]" (2011), she became the third woman to top the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] in three decades. In 2019, Spears withdrew from regular concert touring to focus on her [[Britney Spears conservatorship case|conservatorship case]], leading to the #FreeBritney movement and the 2021 documentary ''[[Framing Britney Spears]]''. Also in 2021, the conservatorship was dissolved after she publicly testified against her family and management for maltreatment. Her 2023 memoir, ''[[The Woman in Me (memoir)|The Woman in Me]]'', debuted atop [[The New York Times Best Seller list|''The New York Times'' Best Seller]] list.

Spears is one of the [[List of best-selling music artists|best-selling music artists of all time]], with estimated sales of over 150 million records. ''[[Forbes]]'' named her as the world's [[Forbes list of the world's highest-paid musicians#Female|highest-paid female musician]] in 2000 and 2012. She ranked among the [[Time 100|100 most influential people in the world]] by ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' (2021) and the [[Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century|greatest pop stars of the 21st century]] by ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]'' (2023). Her [[List of awards and nominations received by Britney Spears|accolades]] include one [[Grammy Award]], six [[MTV Video Music Awards]] (including the [[Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award]]), seven [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Awards]] and a star on the [[Hollywood Walk of Fame]]. Outside of music, Spears has launched [[Britney Spears products|numerous products]] over the years, with her 2005 fragrance [[Fantasy (fragrance)|Fantasy]] with [[Elizabeth Arden, Inc.]] generating over $1.5 billion in sales by 2011.
